steps.title: Identify what the blank must do
steps.content: The passage claims female farmers constituted the majority in cultivating specific types of crops, then introduces an example with "for instance." The blank must provide a specific, data-supported case where women clearly outnumber men in a particular region-and-crop pairing.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'most of the farmers who mainly cultivated cereals and most of the farmers who mainly cultivated non–root vegetables in south Ondo were women.': Merges two crop types in one region without isolating a clear majority claim for each. Too broad to serve as a precise "for instance." ✗
  • Choice B — 'more women in central Ondo mainly cultivated root crops than mainly cultivated cereals.': Compares two crop types among women in one region; does not establish that women are the majority of farmers in any category. ✗
  • Choice C — 'most of the farmers who mainly cultivated non–root vegetables in north and south Ondo were women.': Directly confirms female majority in a specific crop category across two regions — exactly the kind of precise data point "for instance" requires. ✓
  • Choice D — 'a relatively equal proportion of women across the three regions of Ondo mainly cultivated cereals.': Describes equal proportions, which contradicts the "majority" claim the example is meant to support. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
